UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT HANFORD-FREUND & CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, BY AUGUST 11, 2014, AS TO PARAGRAPH 26, WHICH IS INCORPORATED INTO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION, INCLUDING CONTRACT-BASED CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT AVAILABLE IN BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MAY RE-ALLEGE THIS PARAGRAPH, IF PROPER, IN TORT CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION IS ALSO GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACT REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS TO A CORPORATE DEFENDANT. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED, COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS TO SUPPORT MALICE, CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF PLAINTIFF RIGHTS AND SAFETY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (AJR/501/RG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Burns, 510-254-6767 Attorney at Law for defendadnt and moving party Hanford-Freund Co. appears via Courtcall.
Michael Schreiber, Bracamamontes & Llasak, 415-835-6777

Argued: Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT HANFORD-FREUND & CO.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is

GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Strike is GRANTED with leave to amend, by August 11, 2014, as to paragraph 26, which is incorporated into all causes of action, including contract-based causes of action. Plaintiff does not dispute that punitive damages are not available in breach of contract causes of action. Plaintiff may re-allege this paragraph, if proper, in tort causes of action. Motion is also GRANTED with leave to amend to allege fact regarding punitive damages as to a corporate defendant. Motion is otherwise DENIED, complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support malice, conscious disregard of plaintiff rights and safety. Order signed in open Court. (AJR/501/rg)
